name: Mirlitons Farcis Aux Crevettes
recipeIngredient: Freshly ground black pepper -- to taste
1/2 cup fresh bread crumbs, finely minced
recipeInstructions:
MINUTES (par-boil 10; prep 10; bake 20)
- Split the chayotes lengthwise in half. Place in a kettle of cold water with salt to taste. Bring to a boil and let simmer about 10 minutes. Do not overcook or the vegetable will become mushy. Drain and briefly rinse under cold water. Drain again. Using a spoon, scoop out the flesh and seeds of each half, leaving a shell about 1/8-inch thick or slightly thicker. Set the shells aside. Chop the flesh and seeds fine. There should be about 1 cup.
- Peel and devein the shrimp and chop coarsely. There should be about 1 1/4 cups. Set aside.
- Preheat the oven to 425 degrees. Melt half the butter in a saucepan and add the onion and garlic. Cook, stirring, until wilted. Sprinkle with flour and stir to distribute evenly. Add the milk, stirring rapidly with a wire whisk. When the sauce is thickened and smooth, add the chopped pulp. Bring to a boil, stirring, and add the ginger and scallions. Remove from the heat and stir in the egg yolk. Let cool to room temperature. Stir in the shrimp, salt, pepper, 1/4 cup of the bread crumbs and the parsley. Use the mixture to stuff chayote halves. Pile up and smooth over.
- Blend the remaining 1/4 cup of bread crumbs and cheese. Sprinkle the tops with the mixture, patting to help it adhere. Dot with the remaining 2 tablespoons butter. Arrange the stuffed halves in a lightly buttered baking dish and bake for 20 minutes.
